description_long: This is a classic black rotary dial corded telephone, featuring a robust rectangular base and a curved handset resting on top. The rotary dial is prominently positioned on the slanted front face, with visible finger holes and number markings. A straight, non-coiled cord extends from the left side of the handset to the base, suggesting a vintage design. The handset is thick and ergonomically shaped, designed to fit comfortably against the ear and mouth. The base is solid and slightly angled, providing stability and a clear view of the dial. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with a glossy black finish typical of mid-20th-century telephones.
description: A vintage black rotary dial corded telephone with a solid rectangular base and a curved handset connected by a straight cord. The rotary dial and glossy finish evoke a classic, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rotary dial, number markings, and the handset resting on the cradle.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the slanted base, the thickness of the handset, and the cord attachment.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the handset and the cord trailing from the base.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the handset profile and the cord extending outward.
